quote_text: What is the nature of the universe, and what is mine--in particular.
reflection: What belongs to the whole, and what belongs to you today?

quote_text: Nothing that is according to nature can be evil.
reflection: What fear can you face by seeing it as part of nature?

quote_text: We ought not to fly into a rage even when the injury appears to be open and distinct.
reflection: Where can restraint protect your judgment before you respond?

quote_text: If law, then are we fellow-citizens.
reflection: How can you act today as a citizen of one common world?

quote_text: Straightway then practise saying to every harsh appearance: You are an appearance, and in no manner what you appear to be.
reflection: What harsh appearance needs examination before belief?

quote_text: His course will not be straightforward; he must go upwards and downwards, be tossed about, and guide his vessel through troubled waters.
reflection: What troubled water can you navigate without surrendering your course?

quote_text: For a man even to love them that transgress against him.
reflection: Who would be harder, and better, to answer with goodwill?

quote_text: Suppose that it passes by you. Do not detain it.
reflection: What passing thing can you let go without reaching after it?

quote_text: No one lays claim to his own self, every one wastes his time over some one else.
attribution: Seneca, On the Shortness of Life II
reflection: Where are you giving away time that should belong to your own purpose?

quote_text: What pain soever thou art in, let this presently come to thy mind, that it is not a thing whereof thou needest to be ashamed.
reflection: What discomfort can you carry without adding shame to it?

quote_text: When we think of this, let us deal more justly with sinners, and believe that those who scold us are right.
reflection: How can remembering your own faults make you fairer to someone else?

quote_text: Take away then aversion from all things which are not in our power.
reflection: What are you resisting that was never yours to command?

quote_text: Blind are their eyes, yea, stopped are their ears to God's Law universal.
reflection: What truth are you too busy or desirous to hear clearly?

quote_text: Not as though thou hadst thousands of years to live.
reflection: What right action becomes urgent when you stop assuming endless time?

quote_text: Labour calls for the best man.
reflection: What duty asks you to honor sacrifice with steadiness today?

quote_text: Duties are universally measured by relations.
reflection: Which relationship defines the duty in front of you?

quote_text: And let silence be the general rule, or let only what is necessary be said, and in few words.
reflection: Where would fewer words show better self-command today?

quote_text: That only which is present, is that, which either of them can lose.
reflection: How would your priorities change if only this present moment were yours?

quote_text: You must pass sleepless nights, endure toil, go away from your kinsmen, be despised by a slave.
reflection: What cost of real progress are you willing to accept?

quote_text: And what does this mean? For it is not furnished with shoes by means of a smith, nor with arms by means of a shoemaker.
reflection: What service is actually yours to give your community?

quote_text: A man's soul doth wrong and disrespect itself first and especially, when as much as in itself lies it becomes an aposteme, and as it were an excrescency of the world.
reflection: Where does resentment separate you from the larger whole?
